does: SDOF presents a framework for multi-agent orchestration that enforces state constraints through a constrained state machine architecture. The system comprises an Online-RLHF Specialized Intent Router trained via Generative Reward Modeling (GRPO) that directs tasks to appropriate agents, paired with a StateAwareDispatcher that validates execution against a GoalStage finite automaton and SkillRegistry with precondition and postcondition checks. Evaluated on a recruitment system with 6000+ enterprises, 185 expert-curated scenarios, and 1671 live API calls, the framework achieves 80.9% joint accuracy on FSM-constrained routing (versus 48.9% for zero-shot GPT-4o) and 86.5% end-to-end task completion while successfully blocking all 22 injection and illegal HR operations.
connects: This work builds on multi-agent orchestration frameworks like LangChain, LangGraph, and CrewAI that use graph-based task routing, extending them with formal verification capabilities absent in prior systems. It combines recent advances in RLHF-based router training with classical finite-state machine theory to enforce business process constraints. The approach draws on Generative Reward Modeling (GRPO) for specializing language models to specific routing tasks, while the precondition/postcondition validation pattern extends classical software engineering practices into agentic systems. The work responds to the growing challenge of alignment tax—the performance cost of safety constraints—in multi-agent systems, particularly for regulated domains like HR where incorrect task routing carries compliance risks.
enables: SDOF establishes a pattern for building provably constrained multi-agent systems applicable across regulated industries including finance, healthcare, and legal services. The framework enables future work on automated constraint extraction from business process documentation and on scaling state-aware dispatch to more complex automata. It points toward hybrid approaches combining learned routing with formal verification, potentially enabling certification of agentic system behavior for compliance auditing. The demonstrated effectiveness of GRPO-trained routers on adversarial FSM-constrained benchmarks opens research directions in specializing foundation models for safety-critical orchestration tasks and in developing better metrics for measuring alignment-tax reduction in multi-agent settings.
